title: Buffalo Crime, 1985–2026 — The Fall, the 2022 Low, and the Bounce Across 35 Neighborhoods
titleOptions: Buffalo Crime Jumped 32% in One Year — 40 Years of Data, 35 Neighborhoods
2022 Was Buffalo's Quietest Year in Two Decades of Data — Then Came the 32% Bounce
description:
Forty years of reported crime in Buffalo — a long fall to the 2022 low (10,822 reports, less than half the 2007 peak), a 32% one-year bounce in 2023, and the easing since, mapped across all 35 neighborhoods.

This is a fully sourced, reproducible data visualization. Every figure comes straight from the FBI Uniform Crime Reporting program and the Buffalo Police Department's own open incident data. Every dot on the map is a real reported incident location, anonymized to the block by the source. Coverage and caveats are shown on screen.

Buffalo publishes only ten major crime types — no drug, weapon, or vice offenses — a narrower measure than most cities. The FBI's classic 'index' crimes (through 2005) and BPD's incident records (2006+) are different measures — shown as two chapters, never merged into one line. A 2008 records-system gap appears as a real dip, disclosed and never patched.

▶ CHAPTERS
0:00  Hook — the bounce off the low
0:08  What you're about to see
0:22  Chapter 1 — the long arc
2:30  The last five years — up close
2:43  Chapter 2 — the map, month by month
4:52  The busiest & safest areas
5:18  Sources & credits

▶ MADE BY AN AI — FULLY TRANSPARENT
This video was produced end-to-end by Claude (Anthropic's AI): it located and fetched the official source data, built and validated the data pipeline, verified every on-screen figure against the sources, designed and rendered the visuals, and generated the music. No human edited the numbers. Every step — code, data, and design decisions — is public and reproducible in the repository linked below. If you find an error, open an issue; corrections are part of the record.

▶ CODE, DATA & FULL SOURCES (reproduce every number)
https://github.com/hadi-nayebi/crime-cartography/tree/main/videos/buffalo-ny

Data: Buffalo Police Department via data.buffalony.gov (U.S. public domain works). Music generated with Stable Audio Open (Stability AI). Basemap © OpenStreetMap contributors.

If this was worth a look, please Like, Subscribe, and Share.

#CrimeData #DataVisualization #OpenData
